ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "login" TEXT /* Login username of the employee */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the employee */,
  "salary" TEXT /* Current salary of the employee */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date when the employee started working */,
  "email" TEXT /* Employee's email address */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Employee's phone number */,
  "address" TEXT /* Employee's home address */,
  "emergency_contact" TEXT /* Contact information for an emergency contact */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Employee's date of birth */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Employee's gender */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the employee belongs to */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role the employee has */,
  "status" TEXT /* Employment status (e.g., Active, On Leave, Terminated) */,
  "supervisor_id" TEXT /* ID of the employee's direct supervisor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "manager_id" TEXT /* ID of the manager in charge of the department */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the department */,
  "budget" TEXT /* Annual budget for the department */,
  "headcount" INTEGER /* Number of employees in the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "payroll" (
  "payroll_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each payroll record */,
  "employee_id" TEXT /* Reference to the employee's ID */,
  "salary" TEXT /* Salary for the pay period */,
  "pay_date" TEXT /* Date when the salary was paid */,
  "deductions" TEXT /* Deductions applied for the pay period */,
  "bonus" TEXT /* Bonus amount for the pay period */,
  "net_salary" TEXT /* Net salary after deductions and bonuses */,
  "tax" TEXT /* Tax amount deducted from the salary */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Method of payment (e.g., Direct Deposit, Check) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("payroll_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_payroll_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance review */,
  "employee_id" TEXT /* Reference to the employee's ID */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date when the review was conducted */,
  "score" INTEGER /* Score assigned during the review */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or notes from the reviewer */,
  "reviewer_id" TEXT /* ID of the reviewer */,
  "review_period" TEXT /* Period for which the review is conducted (e.g., Q1, Q2) */,
  "performance_metrics" TEXT /* Specific performance metrics used for the review */,
  "improvement_areas" TEXT /* Areas for improvement identified during the review */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_reviews_reviewer_id FOREIGN KEY ("reviewer_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_reviews_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role's responsibilities */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department associated with the role */,
  "responsibilities" TEXT /* Detailed responsibilities of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_roles_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_roles" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the mapping */,
  "employee_id" TEXT /* Reference to the employee's ID */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the role's ID */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_roles_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "attendance" (
  "attendance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the attendance record */,
  "employee_id" TEXT /* Reference to the employee's ID */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of attendance or absence */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of attendance (e.g., Present, Absent, Sick) */,
  "in_time" TEXT /* Time the employee clocked in */,
  "out_time" TEXT /* Time the employee clocked out */,
  "hours_worked" TEXT /* Total hours worked on the day */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attendance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attendance_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "benefits" (
  "benefit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each benefit record */,
  "employee_id" TEXT /* Reference to the employee's ID */,
  "benefit_type" TEXT /* Type of benefit (e.g., Health Insurance, Retirement Plan) */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value or amount of the benefit provided */,
  "effective_date" TEXT /* Date when the benefit became effective */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Date when the benefit expires */,
  "provider" TEXT /* Name of the benefit provider */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("benefit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_benefits_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the access record */,
  "employee_id" TEXT /* Reference to the employee's ID */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access granted (e.g., Admin, User) */,
  "access_level_id" INTEGER /* ID of the access level */,
  "access_description" TEXT /* Description of the access level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_access_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("access_level_id") REFERENCES access_levels ("access_level_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_levels" (
  "access_level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the access level */,
  "access_level_name" TEXT /* Name of the access level */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the access level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_level_id")
)
